text: 2021 Policy Agenda — Virtual Hunger Action Lobby Week
COVID-19 Response & Recovery — Fight Hunger & Poverty!
● Bolster the Emergency Food System in the COVID-19 Crisis & Beyond: 1) $110 million one-time: Emergency
Food Boxes (Sen. Laird, Asm. Wicks); 2) $32 million one-time: Climate & Disaster Resilience (Sen. Newman, Asm.
Villapudua); and 3) $150 million one-time: Capacity Enhancements
● SB 240 (Eggman) Farm to Food Bank Tax Credit - Support farmers and reduce food waste by extending the tax
credit for donations to 2027 for healthy California grown foods (budget request by Sen. Eggman, Asm. Levine).
● AB 221 (Santiago) - Provide immediate, one-time Emergency Food Benefits for immigrants adversely affected by
the COVID-19 Pandemic and excluded from federal aid because of immigration status. (also budget request).
● SB 464 (Hurtado) Food for All Campaign - Create an equitable nutrition safety net by providing permanent
nutrition benefits for all those ineligible for CalFresh solely due to immigration status (also budget request).
● Enact the vision outlined in the Master Plan for Aging to Prevent Poverty and Hunger for SSI recipients: 1)
Include SSI recipients in all future Golden State Stimulus payments; 2) Restore SSP grants so that they meet the
Elder Economic Index; and 3) Fulfill the “hold harmless” goal of the Supplemental Nutrition Benefit and
Transitional Nutrition Benefit by preventing recipients from churning off the program and by providing
● SB 107 (Wiener): CalFresh—Simpler for Seniors - Simplify the CalFresh application for older adults and people
with disabilities. Make it easier to complete the application and other forms by phone (also budget request).
● SB 364 (Skinner) School Meals for All – Make significant strides toward a hunger-free future for our children.
Provide free school meals to all students without burdensome applications, shame, or stigma. Support farmers
through grants for school nutrition workers to prepare fresh California foods. Establish the Better Out of School
Time (BOOST) program to ensure children have food aid when schools are closed during breaks and disasters.
• California Nutrition Incentive Program (Budget Request) $20 million in additional supplemental benefits
for CalFresh participants purchasing fresh fruit and vegetables at Certified Farmers’ Markets.
For 27 years, California Hunger Action Coalition (CHAC) has been California’s premier anti-hunger coalition: a
broad-based membership organization that unites the foremost statewide & regional anti-hunger groups.
CHAC believes access to adequate, nutritious and safe food is a fundamental human right.
